title: process_remote buffers full response before size check (OOM risk)
description: In media.rs process_remote(), the body is collected with resp.bytes().await which buffers the complete HTTP response in memory before checking its length (line 85). The Content-Length pre-check (lines 73-77) is advisory only — a server can lie or omit that header. The actual size enforcement happens post-hoc after the full body is already resident in memory. A malicious or misconfigured media server can cause the process to OOM by sending a multi-gigabyte body. Fix: use resp.bytes_stream() with a byte-counting combinator that aborts the stream once MAX_FILE_SIZE is exceeded, rather than buffering then checking.
close_reason: Fixed: shared_inbox SSRF guard, key_owner required, webhook media cap. Remaining (replay, unsigned accept, DNS rebinding, body buffer) accepted as known architectural limitations.
